title: Incident timeline / postmortem chronology
description: Data contract for the incident-timeline format: a vertical, chronological PAST event log for an incident / postmortem. Events run top-to-bottom down a time rail with exact timestamps, kind/severity coding and actor attribution; phase markers (detected -> mitigated -> resolved) carry durations; a header states severity + key times; a contributing-factors panel and a sticky summary rail with a LIVE-computed MTTR (time-to-detect / time-to-mitigate / time-to-resolve, derived from the data, never hard-coded) complete the chrome. This is NOT the timeline-roadmap (that is a FORWARD horizontal gantt of planned work); this reads DOWNWARD as a record of what already happened. The #report-data instance in incident_timeline_sample.html is the canonical example. Default theme: plum.

meta.description: Shared report identity block (see _DATA_DRIVEN.md). Brand-neutral: keep {{ORG}}/{{PROJECT}}/{{AUTHOR}} placeholders unless real values are supplied.

org.description: Organisation wordmark shown in header + footer. Placeholder: {{ORG}}.

project.description: Project / system the incident occurred on. Placeholder: {{PROJECT}}.

reportType.description: Artefact type label shown in the eyebrow.

title.description: Incident title — what happened, stated plainly.
examples: Settlement path outage — duplicate-disbursement clearing under retry

subtitle.description: Optional one-line standfirst: the impact / scope summary shown under the title.

date.description: Report issue / review date (YYYY-MM-DD). Distinct from the incident times below.

theme.description: Initial brand theme; set on <html data-theme>. Live-switchable via the .rc-theme dot group. Default plum for this format.

incident.description: Incident-level facts shown in the header and used to compute phase durations / MTTR. All timestamps are ISO-8601 with offset (e.g. 2026-06-19T01:12:00+09:00); the renderer derives every '+Nm since start' and the time-to-detect / mitigate / resolve from these.

severity.description: Incident severity. Drives the header severity badge colour: sev1/sev2 -> risk (bordeaux), sev3 -> hold (amber), sev4/info -> ok.

status.description: Current lifecycle state of the incident at report time.

start.description: When the incident actually began (impact start / time-zero). Anchors every relative '+Nm' offset.

detected.description: When the incident was first detected (alert fired / reported). start->detected = time-to-detect (TTD).

mitigated.description: Optional — when impact was stopped / contained (the bleed stopped). start->mitigated = time-to-mitigate (TTM).

resolved.description: Optional — when the incident was fully resolved. start->resolved = time-to-resolve / MTTR.

impact.description: Optional one-line blast-radius statement (who/what was affected, magnitude).
examples: ~3,400 settlement requests double-posted; JPY 0 net customer loss after reversal.

actors.description: The people / teams / systems that appear as event authors. Each event's `actor` MUST reference one of these `key`s. Rendered as filter chips in the rail and as the attribution label on each event.

label.description: Display name of the actor (person, team, or system).

kind.description: Optional actor classification (chooses the chip icon: person / team / automated system).

events.description: The chronological event log — the spine of the format. SHOULD be provided in time order (the renderer also sorts by `ts`). Each event sits on the vertical time rail with its exact timestamp and a derived '+Nm since start'. Events whose `kind` matches a phase (detect/resolve) and the mitigate point also act as the phase markers.

ts.description: Exact event timestamp (ISO-8601 with offset). Used for ordering, the rail position, the displayed clock and the '+Nm since start' offset.

actor.description: Author of this event — must match an entry in actors[].key.

kind.description: Event category, drives the node icon + accent: detect (alert/observation), action (a change made), comms (a communication / status update), escalation (page / sev raise), decision (a call made on the bridge), resolve (a milestone toward closure / recovery).

severity.description: Optional per-event severity emphasis (colours the node). Use sparingly for the events that mattered. sev1/sev2 -> risk, sev3 -> hold, info -> ok.

title.description: One-line summary of the event — what happened, in the past tense.

detail.description: Optional longer explanation revealed when the event is expanded. May contain backtick `code` spans (rendered as <code>; all other markup is escaped).

command.description: Optional command / change exhibit for an action-kind event (the change that was made); rendered in a dark mono block inside the expanded detail. May contain newlines.
examples: kubectl -n payments set env deploy/settle-orchestrator RETRY_BUDGET_MS=15000

ref.description: Optional reference chip for this event (ticket / commit / dashboard / runbook). Rendered as a mono ref-chip; href is shown as a non-navigating label in the self-contained sample.

href.description: Optional URL the reference points to (shown as a label; not auto-fetched).

factors.description: Contributing factors / what went wrong — rendered in a dedicated panel below or beside the timeline (NOT on the rail). Each is a labelled cause with a short explanation.

label.description: Short name of the contributing factor.

detail.description: One-to-two sentence explanation of how this factor contributed. May contain backtick `code` spans.

kind.description: Optional factor classification for the leading icon: cause (a root/contributing cause), gap (a missing control / detection gap), trigger (the proximate trigger).

meta.title: Settlement path outage — duplicate disbursements clearing under retry
meta.subtitle: SEV1 — a post-commit retry gap double-posted settlement requests for 1h47m until a DB uniqueness constraint stopped the bleed.
meta.author: {{AUTHOR}}

incident.impact: ~3,400 settlement requests double-posted; JPY 0 net customer loss after automated reversal.

events.title: Settlement throughput dipped; first double-post written to the ledger.
events.detail: Impact start (time-zero). The first duplicate `ledger_entries` row was committed when a client retry fired after a successful-but-slow commit.

events.title: Duplicate-disbursement alert fired (dup_count non-zero).
events.detail: Detection. The `OR-CTL-118-dup` reconciliation alert tripped 19 minutes after impact start — detection lag was the single largest slice of the timeline.

events.title: Paged Incident Commander and raised severity to SEV1.
events.detail: Confirmed live double-posting on the settlement path; customer-facing money movement in scope, so severity was raised from SEV2 to SEV1 and a bridge opened.

events.title: Declared blast radius; agreed to stop the bleed at the database before reversing.
events.detail: Decision on the bridge: prioritise stopping further duplicate persists over reversing existing ones, to bound the blast radius first.

events.title: Raised client retry budget to shrink the post-commit gap window.
events.detail: Partial relief only — narrowed but did not close the race. Rollout completed across all pods.
events.command: kubectl -n payments set env deploy/settle-orchestrator RETRY_BUDGET_MS=15000

events.title: Posted customer-facing status note and internal SEV1 update.
events.detail: Status page updated to 'investigating'; stakeholders notified with the agreed blast-radius statement.

events.title: Built unique index on (account_id, request_id) — duplicate persists stopped.
events.detail: Mitigation point — the bleed stopped here. The CONCURRENTLY build succeeded because the existing duplicates did not overlap the index key after the throughput dip.
events.command: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX CONCURRENTLY ux_ledger_req ON ledger_entries(account_id, request_id);

events.title: Ran the reversal job for the 3,400 confirmed duplicate ledger pairs.
events.detail: Reversals are themselves logged and idempotent on `request_id`, so a re-run is safe. Reconciliation confirmed JPY 0 net customer impact.

events.title: Dashboards green for 15 min; severity lowered and incident resolved.
events.detail: Resolution. `settle_error_rate` and `dup_count` flat at baseline for 15 minutes; handed off to the post-incident review.

factors.label: Retry without idempotency key
factors.detail: The settlement client retried on a slow-but-successful commit; the orchestrator had no idempotency key on `request_id`, so the retry produced a second `ledger_entries` row.

factors.label: Missing database uniqueness constraint
factors.detail: There was no unique index on `(account_id, request_id)`, so the database accepted the duplicate persist instead of rejecting it — the control that finally stopped the bleed had simply never been added.

factors.label: Detection lag
factors.detail: Reconciliation ran on a 6-hour cadence rather than same-day, so 19 minutes of double-posting accumulated before the alert fired — the largest single slice of the timeline.

factors.label: Latency spike on the commit path
factors.detail: A brief storage-layer latency spike pushed commit times past the client retry threshold, which is what set the duplicate-on-retry race in motion.
